name: Remembering Eleanor
based_on: A realistic, evergreen example modeled on a common real-world need — the kind of dignified tribute page families build to remember a loved one and share service details.
summary: A quiet, dignified tribute: a framed portrait with dates and epitaph, a life story with a timeline, a black-and-white photo gallery with a lightbox, service and donation details, and a working memory wall that saves and persists visitors' messages.
sections: Hero — framed portrait, name, years, and epitaph
Life story with drop cap, pull-quote, and a milestone timeline
Photo gallery in a click-to-enlarge lightbox with captions and keyboard nav
Service & celebration cards (service, reception, live stream) with directions
Memory wall — seeded memories plus a validating form that persists via localStorage
Soft footer with a candle motif
setup_questions: Whose memorial is this — their full name, the years (born–passed), and a short epitaph or line they lived by?
Share a short life story and 5–6 photos or moments worth featuring.
What service or celebration details should I include — date, time, place, reception, live stream, and any donation request in lieu of flowers?
